page: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-8 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine configured with 8 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4 GB of memory per core. The instance is powered by an Intel processor featuring 4 physical cores and 2 threads per core with dedicated CPU allocation. The processor architecture includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, supporting advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and Advanced Vector Extensions.

Performance benchmarks place this server in the average tier for most compute and memory tasks. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1770.0 and a multi-core score of 6838.0, alongside a static web serving throughput of 12.95 GB/sec. While text processing performance is strong, scoring in the top 25% on Geekbench, text generation performance on a medium 7B LLM model is weak, yielding 6.31 tokens/sec. Memory read-only bandwidth reaches 530.44 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 61.33 GB/sec for large uncached blocks.

This server configuration does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, and it lacks GPU acceleration. The absence of bundled storage and accelerators makes it a cost-efficient option for workloads that do not require high-speed local disks or parallel graphics processing. It is suited for standard enterprise workloads, including static web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-8 is an x86_64 general-purpose server configured with 8 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel processor with 4 physical cores and hyper-threading, it features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks generally fall within the average range, including a Geekbench single-core score of 1770.0 and multi-core score of 6838.0. It demonstrates strong text processing capabilities but exhibits weak performance in medium-sized LLM text generation. The server is suited for standard enterprise workloads, including static web serving, software compilation, and database operations, offering a balanced resource profile without the overhead of specialized hardware.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-8 is an x86_64 general-purpose server with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and dedicated Intel processors. It supports AVX-512 and AMX instructions for balanced enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-8 is a general-purpose server featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and dedicated Intel processors with AVX-512 support.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel processors and advanced instruction sets for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM with dedicated Intel CPU allocation
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets
Strong text processing performance in benchmark testing
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations
